name: Magaluf and Santa Ponça — circular via Cap de Cala Figuera
name_original: Magaluf y Santa Ponça — circular por el Cap de Cala Figuera
region_intro: Touring cycling around Calvià traverses a diverse landscape on Mallorca, characterized by the majestic Serra de Tramuntana mountains and over 30 miles (50 km) of varied coastline. The region features significant elevation changes in its mountainous areas, alongside serene Mediterranean environments with olive groves and traditional villages. This blend offers cyclists routes ranging from challenging mountain ascents to tranquil coastal stretches and inland paths. Best touring cycling routes around Calvià The most popular touring cycling route is Magaluf and Santa Ponça — circular via Cap de Cala Figuera, an 18.8 miles (30 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes through popular resort towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Mirador de Ricard Roca – Torre del Verger loop from Santa Ponsa, a difficult 52.6 miles (85 km) path. It leads through the Tramuntana mountains, featuring significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Local touring cyclists also love the Esporles Square – Es Grau Pass (468 m) loop from Santa Ponsa, a 39.4 miles (63 km) trail leading through mountain passes, often completed in about 4 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Calvià is defined by challenging mountain passes, scenic coastal roads, and routes through charming traditional villages. The network offers options for various ability levels. The routes in Calvià are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 10,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Calvià's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Calvià?
A: Calvià offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 110 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Calvià has over 30 easy touring cycling routes. These often include scenic coastal roads and paths through serene Mediterranean landscapes, such as sections of the Passeig de Calvià. For a moderate option that offers coastal views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003999483/magaluf-and-santa-ponca-circular-via-cap-de-cala-figuera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magaluf and Santa Ponça — circular via Cap de Cala Figuera&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: What kind of challenging routes can experienced touring cyclists find in Calvià?
A: Experienced touring cyclists will find over 40 difficult routes, particularly in the Serra de Tramuntana mountains. These routes feature significant elevation changes and offer panoramic vistas. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22063769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Es Camp de Mar – Scenic descent with mountain view loop from Urbanització Galatzó&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial elevation gain and mountain views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Calvià?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Calvià are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17417166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Ricard Roca – Torre del Verger loop from Santa Ponsa&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the Tramuntana mountains, offering a comprehensive experience of the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks I can expect to see on a touring cycling route?
A: Calvià's routes offer numerous scenic viewpoints and cultural landmarks. You might encounter traditional Mallorcan villages, olive groves, and stunning sea views along the coast. For breathtaking mountain vistas, routes leading towards the Serra de Tramuntana are ideal. Highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/140523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de Galatzó Summit&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/233522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from the ascent to Puig de Galatzó&lt;/a&gt; offer spectacular panoramas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Calvià?
A: Calvià, like much of Mallorca, enjoys a favorable climate for cycling most of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for exploring. Even in winter, the weather is often mild enough for comfortable rides, while summer can be warm, best suited for early morning or late afternoon excursions.

Q: Where can I find bike rental services in Calvià?
A: Calvià and the wider Mallorca region are well-equipped for cycling tourism, meaning you'll find numerous bike rental shops in towns like Paguera, Santa Ponsa, and Magaluf. These shops often offer a range of touring bikes suitable for the local terrain.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass through traditional villages or cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Calvià weave through picturesque traditional Mallorcan villages such as s'Arracó, es Capdellà, and Calvià itself. These rides offer a chance to see stately homes, old mills, and historic oratories, providing a rich cultural dimension to your cycling experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Calvià?
A: The touring cycling routes around Calvià are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 1,300 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, well-maintained paths, and the variety of routes catering to all abilities, from coastal rides to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes using public transport in Calvià?
A: Calvià is conveniently located with good transport links. While specific public transport access points for every trailhead vary, the municipality is just 20 minutes from the airport and 15 minutes from Palma, the capital, making it relatively accessible. Local bus services connect many towns and villages, which may facilitate reaching certain starting points.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options around Calvià?
A: Yes, Calvià offers several longer touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32436476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C'an Pere Antoni Beach – View of Palma loop from Costa de la Calma&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 57 kilometers, offering a substantial ride with views towards Palma. Routes extending into the Serra de Tramuntana can also be quite long and demanding.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes with opportunities for cafe stops?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Calvià pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes and restaurants. The region's excellent cycling tourism infrastructure means that refreshment stops are generally easy to find, especially along coastal roads and in traditional villages, allowing for pleasant breaks during your ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Calvià?
A: You can expect a highly diverse terrain. The region features challenging mountain passes in the Serra de Tramuntana, offering significant elevation changes. Along the coast, you'll find gentler, more tranquil roads with stunning sea views. Inland, routes traverse serene Mediterranean landscapes with olive groves and charming traditional villages, providing a varied and engaging cycling experience.
